chief_complaint: A 34-year-old woman presented with progressive quadriparesis, bilateral ptosis, and facial palsy developing over one month.
history: Neuropsychiatric systemic lupus erythematosus (NPSLE) manifesting as Guillain-Barre syndrome (GBS) is exceptionally rare. The patient had marked B-cell dysregulation with elevated immunoglobulin G, positive antinuclear antibodies, anti-double-stranded DNA antibodies, anti-ribosomal P antibodies, and hypocomplementemia. She had inadequate response to intravenous immunoglobulin monotherapy.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Laboratory investigations revealed: immunoglobulin G (24.30 g/L), antinuclear antibodies (1:320), anti-double-stranded DNA antibodies (128 IU/ml), anti-ribosomal P antibodies (450 AU/ml), hypocomplementemia (C3 1.10 g/L, C4 0.28 g/L), cerebrospinal fluid analysis (protein 1314 mg/L, cells 1x10(6)/L), and nerve conduction studies (acute inflammatory demyelinating polyradiculoneuropathy).
